records.ety: [OE. wandren, wandrien, AS. wandrian; akin to G. wandern to wander; fr. AS. windan to turn. See Wind to turn.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wandered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wandering.]

defs: To ramble here and there without any certain course or with no definite object in view; to range about; to stroll; to rove; as, to wander over the fields.
quotes: They wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins.

defs: To go away; to depart; to stray off; to deviate; to go astray; as, a writer wanders from his subject.
quotes: When God caused me to wander from my father's house.
O, let me not wander from thy commandments.

defs: To be delirious; not to be under the guidance of reason; to rave; as, the mind wanders.
To travel over without a certain course; to traverse; to stroll through.
